title: Lesson 30: FINAL BOSS — Deploy Your Website (Netlify / Vercel)

content.explanation:
🚀 You Are Now a Real Developer

You have built websites using HTML, styled them with CSS, and learned how real projects work.

Now it is time for the final step: putting your website online.

🌐 What is Deployment?

Deployment means making your website accessible to anyone in the world using a link.

⚡ Platforms You Will Use

  • Netlify
  • Vercel
  • GitHub Pages

🔥 This is what real developers do every day

syntax_breakdown.content:
🧪 Deployment Steps (Simple Workflow)

1. Create GitHub repository
2. Upload your project files
3. Go to Netlify or Vercel
4. Connect your GitHub repo
5. Click Deploy
6. Get your live website link

📌 Example Result

Your website becomes:

https://your-project-name.netlify.app

🧠 Key Idea

👉 Code locally → Push to GitHub → Deploy online

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
My First Live Website

This project will soon be online for the world to see.

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 FINAL MISSION:

1. Imagine your project is ready for deployment
2. Write a page that includes:
   - A title saying 'My Deployed Website'
   - A paragraph describing your project
   - A list of files in your project
3. Add a note saying: 'Ready for GitHub and Netlify deployment'

🔥 This is your graduation moment

quiz_engine.question: What is the main purpose of deploying a website?
options: To store code locally on your computer
To make your website accessible online to users
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 Deployment makes your website live and accessible on the internet using platforms like Netlify or Vercel.

takeaways: Deployment makes websites live on the internet
You have completed your beginner web journey

cheat_sheet.vercel: Modern deployment platform for web apps
